entries.title: OpenAI’s existential questions
entries.description: On the latest episode of Equity, we discuss OpenAI's latest acquisitions and whether they address "two big existential problems" for the company.

entries.title: The 12-month window
entries.description: A lot of AI startups exist partly because the foundation models haven't expanded into their category yet. As many jokingly acknowledge, that won't last forever.

entries.title: Palantir posts mini-manifesto denouncing inclusivity and ‘regressive’ cultures
entries.description: Palantir's ideological bent has come under more scrutiny as it's worked with ICE and positioned itself as a defender of "the West."

entries.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Uber enters its assetmaxxing era
entries.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

entries.title: Tesla brings its robotaxi service to Dallas and Houston
entries.description: The company now offers robotaxi service in three cities, all of them in Texas, after launching in Austin last year and starting to offer rides without safety drivers in January 2026.

entries.title: AI chip startup Cerebras files for IPO
entries.description: In recent months, the company announced an agreement with Amazon Web Services to use Cerebras chips in Amazon data centers, as well as a deal with OpenAI reportedly worth more than $10 billion.

entries.title: Anthropic’s relationship with the Trump administration seems to be thawing
entries.description: Despite recently being designated a supply-chain risk by the Pentagon, Anthropic is still talking to high-level members of the Trump administration.

entries.title: The App Store is booming again, and AI may be why
entries.description: New data from Appfigures shows a swell of new app launches in 2026, suggesting AI tools could be fueling a mobile software boom.

entries.title: Anthropic takes $5B from Amazon and pledges $100B in cloud spending in return
entries.description: Amazon has made another circular AI deal: It's investing another $5 billion in Anthropic. Anthropic has agreed to spend $100 billion on AWS in return.

entries.title: Google rolls out Gemini in Chrome in 7 new countries

entries.title: It’s not just one thing — it’s another thing

entries.title: NSA spies are reportedly using Anthropic’s Mythos, despite Pentagon feud
entries.description: NSA is said to be using Anthropic's restricted Mythos AI model.

entries.title: CEO and CFO suddenly depart AI nuclear power upstart Fermi
entries.description: The startup, co-founded by former U.S. Energy Secretary Rick Perry, has faced headwinds with its AI campus in Texas.

entries.title: Sam Altman’s project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder.
entries.description: World, which has raised eyebrows (but also a lot of interest) with its Orb-centered anonymous verification project, is looking to expand its influence via a bevy of new partnerships.

entries.title: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles exit OpenAI as company continues to shed ‘side quests’
entries.description: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles are leaving OpenAI as the company shuts down Sora and folds its science team, signaling a sharp pivot away from consumer moonshots toward enterprise AI.

entries.title: Sources: Cursor in talks to raise $2B+ at $50B valuation as enterprise growth surges
entries.description: Returning backers a16z and Thrive are expected to lead the round.

entries.title: ‘Tokenmaxxing’ is making developers less productive than they think
entries.description: There's a lot more code — but it's a lot more expensive and requires a lot more rewriting.

news.title: OpenAI’s existential questions
news.description: On the latest episode of Equity, we discuss OpenAI's latest acquisitions and whether they address "two big existential problems" for the company.

news.title: The 12-month window
news.description: A lot of AI startups exist partly because the foundation models haven't expanded into their category yet. As many jokingly acknowledge, that won't last forever.

news.title: Palantir posts mini-manifesto denouncing inclusivity and ‘regressive’ cultures
news.description: Palantir's ideological bent has come under more scrutiny as it's worked with ICE and positioned itself as a defender of "the West."

news.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Uber enters its assetmaxxing era
news.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

news.title: Tesla brings its robotaxi service to Dallas and Houston
news.description: The company now offers robotaxi service in three cities, all of them in Texas, after launching in Austin last year and starting to offer rides without safety drivers in January 2026.

news.title: AI chip startup Cerebras files for IPO
news.description: In recent months, the company announced an agreement with Amazon Web Services to use Cerebras chips in Amazon data centers, as well as a deal with OpenAI reportedly worth more than $10 billion.

news.title: Anthropic’s relationship with the Trump administration seems to be thawing
news.description: Despite recently being designated a supply-chain risk by the Pentagon, Anthropic is still talking to high-level members of the Trump administration.

news.title: The App Store is booming again, and AI may be why
news.description: New data from Appfigures shows a swell of new app launches in 2026, suggesting AI tools could be fueling a mobile software boom.

news.title: Anthropic takes $5B from Amazon and pledges $100B in cloud spending in return
news.description: Amazon has made another circular AI deal: It's investing another $5 billion in Anthropic. Anthropic has agreed to spend $100 billion on AWS in return.

news.title: Google rolls out Gemini in Chrome in 7 new countries
news.description: Google is rolling out Gemini in Chrome in Australia, Indonesia, Japan, the Philippines, Singapore, South Korea, and Vietnam. The company is rolling this feature out to both desktop and iOS in all of t

news.title: It’s not just one thing — it’s another thing

news.title: NSA spies are reportedly using Anthropic’s Mythos, despite Pentagon feud
news.description: NSA is said to be using Anthropic's restricted Mythos AI model.

news.title: CEO and CFO suddenly depart AI nuclear power upstart Fermi
news.description: The startup, co-founded by former U.S. Energy Secretary Rick Perry, has faced headwinds with its AI campus in Texas.

news.title: Sam Altman’s project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder.
news.description: World, which has raised eyebrows (but also a lot of interest) with its Orb-centered anonymous verification project, is looking to expand its influence via a bevy of new partnerships.

news.title: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles exit OpenAI as company continues to shed ‘side quests’
news.description: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles are leaving OpenAI as the company shuts down Sora and folds its science team, signaling a sharp pivot away from consumer moonshots toward enterprise AI.

news.title: Sources: Cursor in talks to raise $2B+ at $50B valuation as enterprise growth surges
news.description: Returning backers a16z and Thrive are expected to lead the round.

news.title: ‘Tokenmaxxing’ is making developers less productive than they think
news.description: There's a lot more code — but it's a lot more expensive and requires a lot more rewriting.

news.title: Tokenmaxxing, OpenAI’s shopping spree, and the AI Anxiety Gap

news.title: Anthropic launches Claude Design, a new product for creating quick visuals
news.description: The company says Claude Design is intended to help people like founders and product managers without a design background share their ideas more easily.
